message:
fcp-crypto: add metamorphic relation test suite

Ten property-based MRs covering the algebraic surface of the crypto
primitives where oracle values are unknown but input→output
relationships are contractually fixed:

  MR 1  hpke_open(sk, hpke_seal(pk, m, aad), aad) == Ok(m)
  MR 1b hpke_open(sk, sealed_under_aad_A, aad_B) must reject
  MR 2  pk.verify(m, sk.sign(m)) is Ok
  MR 2b pk.verify(m',  sk.sign(m)) is Err for m != m'
  MR 2c pk_B.verify(m, sk_A.sign(m)) is Err for sk_A != sk_B
  MR 3  canonicalize(c) == canonicalize(decode(canonicalize(c)))
  MR 4  aead.encrypt_random_nonce(m, k) differs across two calls
  MR 4b hpke_seal(pk, m, aad) produces a fresh ephemeral `enc`
  MR 5  hkdf(salt, ikm, info) is deterministic
  MR 5b hkdf distinguishes distinct info labels

Each block runs 128–256 proptest cases; the suite completes in ~1.65 s
with `cargo test -p fcp-crypto --test metamorphic` on the existing
/tmp/fcp-p6-r1 target dir.

These are the properties that plain unit tests miss: a seeded RNG that
collapses to one key, a canonicalizer that isn't idempotent (breaks
ObjectId derivation), an HKDF that picks up stray entropy, an HPKE
impl that caches the ephemeral keypair across seals. Each MR is
orthogonal on the MR-strength matrix (no two detect the same bug
class), so the suite compounds multiplicatively rather than redundantly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
